critiques.summary: Reviewed the Session 2 implementation for Set 092 in the actual repo: the new System Status/webview wiring (`src/providers/CustomSessionSetsView.ts`, `media/session-sets-tree/client.js`, `media/session-sets-tree/systemStatusHtml.js`), watcher plumbing (`src/extension.ts`), the model contract (`src/providers/SessionSetsModel.ts`, `src/types/sessionSetsWebviewProtocol.ts`), and the updated unit/Playwright coverage plus the set artifacts and READMEs. I found one blocking completeness issue in the set artifacts and one material doc/code drift issue.

findings.description: Violation: the set spec says Session 2 "Ends with: ... `close_session` succeeded" and lists "`change-log.md` + standard per-session artifacts" as an end-of-set deliverable, but the set directory still has no `change-log.md` at all. Impact: this is not just a cosmetic omission; it means the delivered artifact set is incomplete at the point the set claims it is ready for close, so a reviewer should not approve merge/close on the current tree. Evidence: `docs/session-sets/092-work-explorer-single-dialect-renderer/spec.md` requires `change-log.md`, `grep` in that set directory finds only references to it, and `read_file` on `docs/session-sets/092-work-explorer-single-dialect-renderer/change-log.md` fails because the file does not exist. Fix: add the required `change-log.md` before close-out, or update the set state/docs so the repository no longer claims this deliverable is complete yet.

Violation: the current docs still claim the Getting Started form itself shows inline prerequisite warnings, but the shipped code removed those form-local warnings and moved them to the shared System Status strip. Impact: operators following the README/extension README will look for warnings in the wrong place during setup and validation, and the set's own relocation claim is undermined by stale public documentation; this is material because the docs are explicitly describing current behavior of the just-changed surface. Evidence: `media/session-sets-tree/gettingStartedHtml.js` no longer renders any `data-gs-warning` blocks, `media/session-sets-tree/systemStatusHtml.js` is now the only renderer for provider/Python/Copilot/workspace faults, and the tests explicitly pin that relocation (`src/test/suite/gettingStartedHtml.test.ts`, suites "diagnostics relocated to System Status", "Python diagnostic relocation", and "Copilot diagnostic relocation"). But `README.md` still says Full "surfaces ... an inline warning when the `copilot` CLI can't be found" and that the form "warns inline when a Python interpreter can't be found"; `tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/README.md` likewise says the form "warns inline when no provider API key is visible" and that a step-1 warning appears for missing Python/Copilot. Fix: update both READMEs to describe the System Status strip as the fault surface instead of inline form warnings.

critiques.summary:
I have reviewed the changes for the Work Explorer's single-dialect renderer, the new System Status strip, and the last-known-good `modules.yaml` rendering logic. The review included inspecting the primary implementation files (`CustomSessionSetsView.ts`, `SessionSetsModel.ts`, and `media/session-sets-tree/client.js`), the new and modified unit tests (`systemStatusHtml.test.ts`, `visibleModules.test.ts`), and the end-to-end Playwright tests (`module-tier.spec.ts`).

The core claims from the summary are substantiated by the code:
1.  The diagnostics logic has been centralized in `CustomSessionSetsView.ts` and is rendered into a new, persistent "System Status" strip. This is confirmed by the new `buildSystemStatus` method and the `systemStatus` property on the webview payload.
2.  The `modules.yaml` parsing is now fault-tolerant. The `buildModules` method in `CustomSessionSetsView.ts` now uses a `lastKnownGoodModules` map to cache valid module structures. If `classifyModulesManifest` returns an invalid state, the view is rendered using the cached version, and a fault is added to the system status payload. This prevents the tree from blanking out on a transient syntax error and correctly implements the "last-known-good" guardrail without overwriting user files.
3.  A "Major" correctness bug found during development (documented in `docs/session-sets/092-work-explorer-single-dialect-renderer/s2-issues.json`) has been fixed. I have verified in `media/session-sets-tree/client.js` that the logic to apply the durable configuration seed from the host now runs *before* the System Status strip is rendered, ensuring the strip and the Getting Started form are built from the same consistent state on the initial paint. The new unit test in `systemStatusHtml.test.ts` correctly pins this source-ordering behavior to prevent regression.

The end-to-end and unit tests appear robust and correctly exercise the new functionality, including the invalid-to-valid `modules.yaml` recovery path. The implementation is complete and correct according to the specification.
